ERBB2.summary: ERBB2 encodes a receptor tyrosine kinase whose amplification drives tumor proliferation and survival, confirming breast tumor identity (PMID:24508693). No clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies; jazzPanda assigns ERBB2 exclusively to c1 (Tumor).

KRT7.summary: KRT7 is a luminal epithelial intermediate filament expressed in the vast majority of invasive breast carcinomas, confirming luminal tumor identity (PMID:15141381); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies here.

SCD.summary: SCD encodes stearoyl-CoA desaturase, a lipid-desaturation enzyme upregulated in breast tumor cells to support membrane synthesis and ferroptosis resistance (PMID:41032417); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies here.

EPCAM.summary: EPCAM is a transmembrane glycoprotein driving epithelial proliferation, adhesion, and signaling, and its overexpression is a hallmark of carcinomas, confirming c1 tumor identity (PMID:32507912).

FOXA1.summary: FOXA1 is a luminal hormone-sensing epithelial identity gene that anchors the luminal breast tumor transcriptional program (PMID:39122969); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies here.

CCND1.summary:
CCND1 encodes Cyclin D1, a G1/S cell-cycle driver that is frequently amplified in breast tumor cells, confirming epithelial tumor identity (PMID:19881546). No clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies here, as `max_gc_corr` equals `pearson`, meaning CCND1 localizes best with c1 and no other cluster competes.

**One-sentence summary:**

CCND1 drives G1/S cell-cycle entry and is a hallmark oncogene of breast tumor epithelial cells (PMID:19881546).

GATA3.summary: GATA3 is a transcription factor driving luminal epithelial differentiation and ER-positive identity in breast tumors (PMID:19059610); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies here.

KRT8.summary: KRT8 is a luminal epithelial cytoskeletal protein that reliably marks breast tumor cells (CK8/18/19-positive, ER-positive lineage) (PMID:18223478); no major specificity caveat applies, as myoepithelial and stromal lineages are KRT8-negative.

LUM.summary: LUM encodes a fibroblast-secreted extracellular matrix proteoglycan that anchors stromal identity across connective tissues (PMID:17490399).

POSTN.summary: POSTN (periostin) is a secreted extracellular matrix protein that defines cancer-associated fibroblast identity and supports stromal remodeling (PMID:38648387). **Specificity caveat:** POSTN is also expressed by partial-EMT tumor cells (PMID:36691359), so strong signal in a spatially mixed region may reflect tumor-stromal co-localization rather than pure CAF identity.

CCDC80.summary: CCDC80 (DRO1) is a secreted stromal protein and tumor-suppressor expressed by fibroblasts in the tumor microenvironment, anchoring its identity as a CAF marker (PMID:35422964). No clear cross-lineage signal was detected, so no specificity caveat applies.

CXCL12.summary: CXCL12 (SDF-1) is a chemokine secreted by cancer-associated fibroblasts that drives tumor-stroma crosstalk, confirming stromal identity in breast cancer (PMID:33603163).

MMP2.summary: MMP2 is a fibroblast-secreted gelatinase that remodels the extracellular matrix, anchoring stromal identity in this panel (PMID:33740019); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies, as its top cluster is c2.

SFRP4.summary: SFRP4 is a secreted Wnt antagonist produced by stromal fibroblasts, marking cancer-associated stroma through extracellular matrix remodeling (PMID:39511287).

PDGFRB.summary: PDGFRB drives mesenchymal/stromal identity as a receptor for PDGF-BB signaling in fibroblasts and pericytes; note it also marks perivascular pericytes, so stromal versus pericyte distinction requires spatial context (PMID:27608497).

PDGFRA.summary: PDGFRA encodes a receptor tyrosine kinase that drives fibroblast and mesenchymal stromal cell identity and survival, making it a canonical stromal marker (PMID:35360851); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies here.

LYZ.summary: LYZ encodes lysozyme, a bacteriolytic enzyme that is a canonical phagocytic myeloid marker, confirming macrophage identity (PMID:11900976).

FCER1G.summary: FCER1G encodes the Fc receptor common gamma chain, an adaptor driving phagocytosis and cytokine production in macrophages (PMID:41994604); note a specificity caveat: the panel annotates it to NK/ILC cells, and it is broadly expressed across innate myeloid and innate lymphoid lineages.

CD68.summary: CD68 is a lysosomal membrane glycoprotein marking phagocytic macrophages and confirming myeloid identity in c3; note it also labels microglia and other activated phagocytes (PMID:28601280).

CD4.summary: CD4 is a co-receptor that identifies helper T cells but is also expressed at low levels on monocytes and macrophages, so its presence in c3 is supportive but not macrophage-specific (PMID:32561858).

FGL2.summary: FGL2 (fibroleukin) is a macrophage-expressed immunosuppressive prothrombinase that marks M2-polarized tumor-associated macrophages (PMID:38612926).

ITGAX.summary: ITGAX (CD11c) encodes a myeloid adhesion integrin marking macrophages and activated myeloid cells broadly; note it is also a canonical dendritic cell marker, so it does not distinguish macrophages from dendritic cells alone (PMID:21885153).

CD163.summary: CD163 is a monocyte/macrophage-restricted hemoglobin-scavenger receptor and established marker of M2-polarized, anti-inflammatory macrophages, with no clear expression in other lineages in this tissue context (PMID:39451197).

FCGR3A.summary: FCGR3A (CD16) encodes a low-affinity IgG Fc receptor that drives antibody-dependent cytotoxicity and is a canonical marker of non-classical monocytes and tissue macrophages; note that NK cells also express FCGR3A, so co-expression with myeloid markers is needed to confirm macrophage identity (PMID:21404581).

SERPINA3.summary: SERPINA3 encodes a serine protease inhibitor expressed in breast glandular epithelial cells, supporting myoepithelial identity; note that it is also upregulated in tumor cells undergoing EMT, so its presence alone does not exclude a tumor origin (PMID:33569740).

DST.summary: DST (dystonin/BPAG1) encodes a hemidesmosomal cytoskeletal linker that anchors intermediate filaments to the basement membrane, a defining structural feature of myoepithelial cells (PMID:24025550). No clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies here, as the panel annotation confirms myoepithelial specificity.

MYLK.summary: c4 is called Myoepithelial at High confidence, driven by MYLK (glm_coef 5.90, pearson 0.83), ACTA2 (glm_coef 5.07, pearson 0.59), KRT14 (glm_coef 4.65, pearson 0.83).

ACTA2.summary: ACTA2 (alpha-smooth muscle actin) encodes the contractile cytoskeletal protein that defines myoepithelial identity in breast ducts (PMID:27195907); caveat: it also marks CAFs and smooth muscle cells, so it is not lineage-exclusive.

KRT14.summary: KRT14 encodes a basal cytoskeletal keratin that defines myoepithelial identity in the breast, though it also marks basal-like tumor cells, so co-expression with luminal markers should be checked to confirm lineage (PMID:38915368).

KRT5.summary: KRT5 encodes a basal cytoskeletal keratin that defines myoepithelial identity in normal breast; note it also marks basal-like tumor cells, so co-expression with stromal or contractile markers is needed to confirm a true myoepithelial call (PMID:16341146).

MYH11.summary: MYH11 encodes smooth muscle myosin heavy chain, a canonical contractile marker of breast myoepithelial cells lining ducts and lobules (PMID:27195907). No specificity caveat: although the panel annotates MYH11 to smooth muscle cells, true vascular smooth muscle is captured by the Endothelial cluster (c7), so strong MYH11 signal in c4 is consistent with myoepithelial identity.

OXTR.summary: OXTR encodes the oxytocin receptor, which drives myoepithelial contraction for milk ejection and defines myoepithelial identity in human breast (PMID:42420002); note that in canine ductal tumors OXTR also appears in luminal cells, so luminal co-expression is a potential specificity caveat.

IL7R.summary: IL7R (CD127) drives T cell survival and homeostatic proliferation, making it a canonical T cell identity marker (PMID:22551764); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies here.

PTPRC.summary: **Specificity caveat applies:** PTPRC (CD45) is a pan-leukocyte phosphatase essential for T cell receptor signaling and broadly marks all leukocytes, including macrophages, B cells, and dendritic cells, not T cells alone (PMID:27598832).

TRAC.summary: TRAC encodes the TCR-alpha constant chain, essential for surface TCR-alphabeta complex assembly and thus definitive T cell identity (PMID:21206088); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies.

CD3E.summary: CD3E encodes the epsilon subunit of the TCR-CD3 signaling complex, making it a T cell-specific surface marker essential for antigen recognition and T cell identity (PMID:25320284). No specificity caveat applies; CD3E is not known to clearly mark any other lineage.

CXCR4.summary: CXCR4 is the CXCL12-driven chemokine receptor directing T cell tissue homing and retention (PMID:34648304); note it is also expressed on B cells and macrophages, so it is not T-cell-exclusive.

CD8A.summary: CD8A encodes the MHC class I co-receptor that defines cytotoxic T lymphocytes, confirming c5 as a CD8+ T cell cluster (PMID:31522417). No clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies; CD8A expression is restricted to cytotoxic T cells and a minor NK subset, not a distinct non-lymphoid lineage.

CCL5.summary: CCL5 (RANTES) is a T cell-secreted chemokine that recruits and retains effector T cells in the tumor microenvironment, supporting c5's T cell identity (PMID:35917973). No clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies here, as CCL5's top cluster assignment is exclusively c5.

IL2RG.summary: IL2RG encodes the common gamma chain shared by IL-2, IL-4, IL-7, IL-15, and IL-21 receptors, making it essential for T cell development and survival (PMID:24771849). **Specificity caveat:** the panel annotates IL2RG to Dendritic cells, and it is broadly expressed across lymphoid and innate immune lineages, so it supports but does not uniquely confirm T cell identity.

MS4A1.summary: MS4A1 (CD20) encodes a B-cell-surface calcium channel essential for B cell activation and proliferation, making it the canonical B cell identity marker (PMID:39650658); note that a rare CD20+ T cell subset exists via trogocytosis, so a small T cell specificity caveat applies.

BANK1.summary: BANK1 is a B-cell-specific scaffold protein that amplifies BCR-induced calcium signaling, making it a reliable B cell identity marker (PMID:11782428).

MZB1.summary: MZB1 is a B cell-specific ER chaperone that supports antibody secretion and calcium homeostasis, confirming B cell identity (PMID:21093319).

CD79A.summary: CD79A (Ig-alpha) is essential for B cell receptor surface expression and survival signaling, making it a definitive B cell identity marker with no cross-lineage specificity concern (PMID:36426942).

CD19.summary: CD19 is a pan-B-cell co-receptor that amplifies BCR signaling and defines committed B lymphocyte identity across all maturation stages, with no meaningful cross-lineage expression (PMID:20207528).

TNFRSF17.summary: TNFRSF17 (BCMA) is a plasma cell survival receptor, confirming antibody-secreting B lineage identity in this cluster (PMID:35144648); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies.

CD79B.summary: CD79B (Igβ) is an obligate B-cell receptor complex component required for surface BCR display and B cell signaling, with no clear cross-lineage expression (PMID:9269755).

AQP1.summary: AQP1 is a water-channel protein that marks capillary endothelia across tissues, supporting endothelial identity; note that in advanced carcinomas AQP1 can also appear in neoplastic tumor cells, so co-expression with other endothelial markers is advisable (PMID:15809704).

PECAM1.summary: PECAM1 (CD31) mediates endothelial cell-cell adhesion at junctions and is a canonical pan-endothelial marker (PMID:17116209); note that platelets and leukocytes also express PECAM1, so strong signal in immune-dense regions warrants cross-cluster review.

VWF.summary: VWF is a canonical endothelial hemostatic glycoprotein stored in Weibel-Palade bodies, confirming endothelial identity, though its expression is heterogeneous across vessel types and absent in some capillary beds (PMID:16234507).

CD93.summary: CD93 is a transmembrane glycoprotein that promotes endothelial cell adhesion and angiogenesis, making it a reliable endothelial identity marker (PMID:31287944).

CAV1.summary: CAV1 encodes the structural caveolae scaffold protein that drives endothelial transcytosis and vascular signaling (PMID:15361787); note that CAV1 also marks macrophages and smooth muscle cells, so it is not endothelial-exclusive.

CLEC14A.summary: CLEC14A is a tumor endothelial C-type lectin that drives pro-angiogenic migration and tube formation, confirming endothelial identity (PMID:21706054); no clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies.

RAMP2.summary: RAMP2 is the adrenomedullin receptor accessory protein essential for vascular endothelial integrity and homeostasis (PMID:23355623). No clear cross-lineage specificity caveat applies; its top cluster is c7 and `max_gc_corr` equals its own `pearson`, confirming it does not localize better with any other cluster. Note that the panel annotates RAMP2 under "Adipocytes," but jazzPanda's spatial signal assigns it firmly to c7 (Endothelial), consistent with the established literature.

MMRN2.summary: MMRN2 is an endothelial-specific extracellular matrix protein that regulates angiogenesis by sequestering VEGFA, confirming endothelial identity (PMID:28435016). Note: the panel annotates MMRN2 under "Adipocytes," so verify no adipocyte signal is present in this dataset.

TCL1A.summary: TCL1A is a proto-oncogene and survival factor expressed in plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDCs), supporting their identity here; however, it is also a canonical B cell marker (panel annotation: B cells), so its specificity for pDC versus B cell lineage requires caution (PMID:12576313).

LILRA4.summary: LILRA4 (ILT7/CD85g) is a pDC-exclusive inhibitory receptor that suppresses type I interferon production, making it a highly specific plasmacytoid dendritic cell identity marker with no clear cross-lineage caveat (PMID:30395816).

SPIB.summary: SPIB is an ETS-family transcription factor required for plasmacytoid and immature lymphoid DC identity, confirming its role as a DC-lineage specifier (PMID:40885192); note that SPIB also drives B-cell development, so strong B-cell co-markers in c8 would warrant a specificity check.

PLD4.summary: PLD4 is a surface phospholipase expressed on plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDCs), supporting Dendritic identity; note a specificity caveat as PLD4 also marks B cells under TLR7/9 stimulation (PMID:37840148).

CPA3.summary: CPA3 encodes a mast-cell-specific granule protease that is a canonical identity marker for tissue-resident mast cells across species (PMID:35983043).

CTSG.summary: CTSG encodes a granule serine protease that marks the MCTC subset of human mast cells; note that neutrophils also express CTSG, so stromal or myeloid contamination could confound the signal (PMID:7721078).
